summary: Professor Lena and policy analyst Marcus engage in a structured debate about immigration policy reform, examining economic, humanitarian, and security dimensions.

title: Consulting a Specialist on Rare Disease Treatment
summary: Dr. Nadia, a rare-disease specialist, consults with patient advocate Raymond about treatment options, clinical trial eligibility, and the ethical complexities surrounding compassionate-use access for a rare metabolic disorder.

summary: Senior counsel Helena and corporate strategist David navigate a high-stakes licensing agreement, addressing indemnification clauses, liability caps, and governing law disputes with precision and strategic foresight.

title: Academic Research Methodology Critique
summary: Research methodologist Professor Sylvia and PhD candidate Ethan engage in a rigorous examination of a proposed mixed-methods study, dissecting sampling strategies, validity threats, and epistemological assumptions.

title: Exploring AI Ethics and the Trolley Problem
summary: Ethicist Professor Iris and AI researcher Daniel explore how classical moral dilemmas like the trolley problem translate into algorithmic decision-making, examining the limitations of encoding ethical frameworks into machine systems.

title: Analyzing Global Supply Chain Disruptions
summary: Supply chain strategist Dr. Priya and trade economist Professor Leon examine the structural vulnerabilities exposed by recent global disruptions, debating reshoring, diversification, and the geopolitics of critical supply chains.

title: Literary Analysis of Post-Colonial Fiction
summary: Literary critic Professor Amara and novelist Theo engage in a nuanced discussion of post-colonial fiction, examining narrative voice, the politics of translation, and whether Western critical frameworks adequately address non-Western literary traditions.

title: Medical Ethics and Patient Autonomy
summary: Bioethicist Dr. Simone and geriatric physician Dr. Marcus navigate a complex case involving a patient's right to refuse life-sustaining treatment, examining the tension between autonomy, beneficence, and clinical duty of care.

title: Philosophical Debate on Technological Determinism
summary: Technology philosopher Professor Celeste and digital sociologist Dr. Owen debate whether technology shapes society in determinate ways, examining hard and soft determinism, agency, and the political implications of how we narrate technological change.

title: Geopolitical Strategy in the Indo-Pacific
summary: Foreign policy analyst Ambassador Claire and defence strategist Dr. James debate the shifting strategic architecture of the Indo-Pacific, examining alliance management, deterrence theory, and the risks of miscalculation in a region of competing great-power interests.

title: Cultural Identity in the Age of Globalization
summary: Cultural anthropologist Dr. Yara and sociologist Professor Felix explore the tensions between global cultural homogenization and the resilience of local identities, examining hybridity, authenticity, and the politics of cultural preservation.

title: Advanced Legal Counsel on Intellectual Property
summary: IP attorney Vivienne advises technology startup founder Nathan on a complex situation involving potential trade secret misappropriation, patent strategy, and the risks of disclosure in competitive markets.

title: Scientific Paradigm Shifts and Peer Review
summary: Philosopher of science Professor Greta and research policy director Dr. Elliot examine how scientific consensus forms and breaks down, the structural limitations of peer review, and the conditions under which revolutionary science emerges.

title: Cybersecurity Strategy for Critical Infrastructure
summary: Chief information security officer Dr. Tessa and national security advisor Dr. Connor develop a framework for defending critical infrastructure against advanced persistent threats, navigating technical, regulatory, and geopolitical dimensions.

title: Corporate Governance and Shareholder Activism
summary: Corporate governance specialist Professor Diane and institutional investor director Malcolm debate the evolving relationship between shareholder activism, board accountability, and long-term corporate value creation versus short-term financial returns.

title: Educational Reform and Standardized Testing
summary: Education policy researcher Dr. Sophie and school reform advocate Professor Jordan debate the merits and limitations of standardized testing, examining how assessment systems shape learning, inequality, and the meaning of educational achievement.

title: Energy Transition Policy Challenges
summary: Energy economist Professor Elena and climate policy strategist Director Hans debate the structural challenges of the energy transition, examining market design, stranded assets, energy security, and the geopolitics of critical minerals.

title: Social Psychology of Conformity and Rebellion
summary: Social psychology professor Diana and graduate student Rafael examine the mechanics of conformity, the conditions enabling principled rebellion, and how social identity shapes group behaviour — drawing on Asch, Milgram, Moscovici, and Janis.

title: Urban Sociology and Gentrification
summary: Urban sociologist Professor Keiko and urban planning consultant Marcus debate the causes and consequences of gentrification — examining displacement, rent gaps, community resistance, right to the city theory, and inclusive urbanism strategies.

title: AI Governance and Existential Risk
summary: AI safety researcher Dr. Priya and technology policy director Leon debate the nature of existential risk from advanced AI, the adequacy of current governance frameworks, international coordination challenges, and the tension between safety and innovation.
